Document:
# Arresting Procedures
Should an Officer order an arrest for a crime, MPs/Law Enforcement MUST arrest that person.
## Arresting a Suspect - Take down the Suspect if they resist and securely restrain them. - Non-lethal force must be used unless the suspect is armed with a lethal weapon and has shown intent to use it. - Move the Suspect to a holding cell.
## Field Arrests These refer to arrests performed in the area of operations. - Anyone may detain personnel of their faction that commit a major or capital crime, but must detain personnel of their faction by orders of the MP/Law Enforcement, commander, or any officer. - Anyone may detain personnel deploying unauthorized, even without orders to do so. - Personnel detained should be passed onto the MP/Law Enforcement or an Officer in a secure area when possible. - MPs/Law Enforcement MAY leave the FOB to detain or arrest members of their faction who have committed any offense that is not minor or optional, but must disengage and return to the FOB if attacked by anyone other than the suspect they are pursuing.
